title: Apinella pumila
intro: Apinella pumila, also known as Dwarf Harebell or Dwarf Campanula, is a small, perennial plant species belonging to the Campanulaceae family. Traditionally, Apinella pumila has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Apinella pumila include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Apinella pumila is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as ointments and salves.
taxonomy: Apinella pumila is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to Europe and North Africa. It is a small plant, growing to a height of about 20-30 cm. This plant is closely related to other members of the genus Apinella. Apinella pumila is often found in dry, sandy, or rocky areas and is known to be a perennial plant. The classification of this plant also places it within the order Apiales and the kingdom Plantae.
